title: Best noise cancelling headphones I've ever owned
review_text: I upgraded from the XM4 and the noise cancellation is noticeably better. The new design is lighter and more comfortable for long wearing sessions. Sound quality is phenomenal with deep bass and clear mids. Battery life easily gets me through a full work week. Worth every penny.
author: JohnT_audio

title: Great but not perfect
review_text: Sound quality and ANC are top tier. My only gripe is that the new headband design doesn't fold flat anymore — the XM4 was much more portable. Also the touch controls sometimes register accidental taps. Still the best in class though.
author: MelanieR

title: Perfect for open office environments
review_text: I work in a noisy open-plan office and these are a lifesaver. The multipoint connection lets me switch between my laptop and phone seamlessly. Call quality is excellent — colleagues say I sound crystal clear on Teams calls.
author: DevOps_Dave

title: Disappointing build quality for the price
review_text: After 3 months the headband started creaking. For $350+ I expected better materials. The plastic feels cheaper than my old XM3. Sound is good but I'm worried about long-term durability. Sony needs to step up their build quality.
author: SkepticalBuyer99

title: Audiophile approved
review_text: As someone who's owned Sennheiser HD660S and Beyerdynamic DT990 Pro, I was skeptical of wireless cans. These surprised me. The LDAC codec delivers near-lossless quality and the tuning is balanced without being boring. Highly recommend for critical listening on the go.
author: AudiophileAnna

title: Good headphones but comfort issues
review_text: Sound and ANC are great. However, the ear cups are too shallow for my ears. After about 90 minutes my ears start hurting because they press against the drivers. I wish Sony made deeper ear cups as an option. Had to return them.
author: BigEarProblems

title: Solid upgrade from Bose 700
review_text: Switched from Bose 700 and the ANC is significantly better. Wind noise handling improved dramatically with the new design. Battery life is longer too. Only giving 4 stars because the Bose app was more intuitive than Sony's Headphones Connect app.
author: FormerBoseUser

title: Terrible Bluetooth connectivity
review_text: Constant dropouts when my phone is in my pocket. Tried resetting, updating firmware, everything. Connection to my MacBook is unstable too. Had 3 replacements from Amazon and all had the same issue. Going back to wired headphones.
author: FrustratedTech

title: Best purchase this year
review_text: These headphones changed how I work. The adaptive noise cancellation adjusts automatically when I walk outside versus sitting at my desk. Speak-to-chat feature is genius — just start talking and the music pauses. Absolutely love them.
author: RemoteWorkerSam

title: Excellent for travel
review_text: Used these on a 14-hour flight and they were incredible. ANC blocked out most engine noise. Comfortable enough to sleep in. Battery lasted the whole trip with ANC on. The quick charge feature (3 hours from 3 minutes) saved me once at the gate.
author: FrequentFlyer_K

title: Microphone is mediocre
review_text: Let me be clear: the sound and ANC are outstanding. But the mic quality for calls is just okay. In quiet rooms it's fine, but in a coffee shop or outdoors, the person on the other end complains about background noise. Not ideal for remote workers who take calls in noisy places.
author: WFH_Manager

title: Replaced my AirPods Max
review_text: Was an AirPods Max user but switched for the better ANC, lighter weight, and USB-C charging. Don't miss the Apple ecosystem integration at all. These pair perfectly with my iPhone via AAC and the app works well on iOS.
author: AppleConvert

title: Not worth the premium over XM4
review_text: I own the XM4 and honestly the upgrade is marginal. Slightly better ANC, slightly different sound. But you lose the folding design and the price is higher. If you already have XM4s, save your money.
author: HonestReviewer2024

title: Life changing for ADHD focus
review_text: I have ADHD and these headphones have genuinely improved my productivity. The noise cancellation creates a bubble of silence that helps me concentrate. I pair them with brown noise and I can finally focus for hours. Best tool in my productivity arsenal.
author: FocusMode_On

title: Great sound held back by the app
review_text: The Sony Headphones Connect app is bloated and slow. Takes forever to load, EQ adjustments lag, and the UI is confusing. The headphones themselves are 5 stars — amazing sound, incredible ANC, great comfort. But the app experience drags it down to 4.
author: UX_Designer_Mike

title: My wife stole them from me
review_text: Bought these for myself. Wife tried them once and now they're hers. Had to buy a second pair. If that's not a glowing review, I don't know what is. She says the lightweight design is the standout feature compared to our old Bose QC35.
author: HappyHusband

title: Good but overheating in summer
review_text: During hot days, the ear cups get uncomfortably warm and sweaty. The synthetic leather doesn't breathe at all. In air-conditioned environments they're perfect, but outdoor use in summer is a no-go. Wish Sony offered a mesh/fabric ear pad option.
author: SummerRunner

title: Best for podcasts and audiobooks
review_text: The voice clarity on these is superb. DSEE Extreme upscaling makes compressed audio sound much better. I listen to 3-4 hours of podcasts daily and these make voices sound natural and present. Bass response is great for music too.
author: PodcastJunkie

title: Left ear driver failed after 2 months
review_text: Sound started crackling in the left ear after 8 weeks. Then it went completely silent. Sony warranty process was painfully slow — took 3 weeks to get a replacement. For a premium product, the QC should be better. Very disappointed.
author: QualityControl101

title: Studio monitor quality on the go
review_text: I'm a music producer and I use these for reference listening when traveling. Flat EQ mode is surprisingly accurate. Not a replacement for my Genelec monitors obviously, but for checking mixes on the go, these are unbeatable.
author: ProducerPete

title: Solid for gaming too
review_text: Low latency mode makes these surprisingly usable for gaming. Used them with my PS5 and the spatial audio is immersive. ANC blocks out the AC and fan noise from my setup. Only wish they had lower latency — there's a tiny delay noticeable in competitive FPS games.
author: GamerGal_92

title: Headband pressure gives me headaches
review_text: Despite the lighter weight, the clamping force is too much for my head. After 45 minutes I get a headache right at the top of my skull. Tried bending and stretching them but it didn't help enough. Ended up going with Bose QC Ultra which fit much better.
author: MigraineMarc

title: Made my commute bearable
review_text: NYC subway commuter here. These block out 90% of the screeching and rumbling. I can finally enjoy music and podcasts without cranking the volume to dangerous levels. The transparency mode is great for hearing announcements too.
author: SubwayRider_NYC

title: Premium feel premium sound
review_text: Unboxing experience was nice. The carrying case is high quality and the headphones feel luxurious. Sound is warm and detailed. ANC is best in class. Only nitpick: I wish the carrying case was slightly smaller since the headphones don't fold anymore.
author: UnboxerSteve

title: Ambient sound mode needs work
review_text: Noise cancellation is excellent but the ambient/transparency mode sounds artificial and processed compared to AirPods Pro 2. It adds a weird electronic haze to environmental sounds. For the price, I expected better transparency mode.
author: ComparisonShopper

title: Helped me through grad school
review_text: Wore these 8+ hours a day in the library while writing my dissertation. Comfortable, long battery life, and the ANC kept all distractions at bay. Literally could not have finished my PhD without them. Buying a second pair as backup.
author: PhDSurvivor

title: Beautiful sound signature
review_text: Warm, slightly V-shaped sound that makes everything enjoyable. Not perfectly flat but that's what makes them fun for casual listening. The LDAC support is a huge plus. Pairing with my Pixel phone gives near-wired quality.
author: AndroidAudioFan

title: Firmware update bricked my pair
review_text: After updating to firmware 3.0.1 the headphones wouldn't turn on. Had to go through a complicated recovery process involving a USB cable and holding buttons in specific sequences. Not acceptable for a consumer product. Works now but I lost trust.
author: TechSupportNightmare

title: Perfect gift for a music lover
review_text: Bought these for my daughter's birthday and she absolutely loves them. She's a classical music student and says the instrument separation is remarkable. The lightweight design means she can practice listening for hours without fatigue.
author: ProudParent

title: Multipoint is a game changer
review_text: Being connected to two devices simultaneously is the feature I didn't know I needed. Watching YouTube on my iPad and instantly taking a call on my phone without touching anything — magical. The switching is seamless about 95% of the time.
author: MultitaskerPro

title: Color options are boring
review_text: Only black and silver? Come on Sony. The midnight blue XM4 was gorgeous. These feel like they were designed by committee. Performance is great but I want headphones that look as good as they sound. Hopefully limited editions are coming.
author: FashionableTech

title: Converted me from wired forever
review_text: I was a wired headphone purist for 15 years. Tried these at a store on a whim and was blown away. The convenience of wireless combined with this level of sound quality is incredible. My HD600s are collecting dust now.
author: WiredConvert

title: Terrible for glasses wearers
review_text: The clamping force presses my glasses frames into my temples painfully. The padding doesn't accommodate glasses at all. Had to return within a week. This is a known issue that Sony refuses to address across multiple generations.
author: GlassesGang

title: Great ANC slightly sibilant highs
review_text: The noise cancellation is the best I've experienced — better than Bose QC Ultra even. However, there's a slight sibilance on certain tracks with female vocals. A small EQ tweak in the app fixes it but it shouldn't be necessary at this price point.
author: DetailOriented

title: Business travel essential
review_text: As a management consultant who flies 3x per week, these are non-negotiable carry items. ANC on planes, crystal clear calls with clients, 30 hour battery life that lasts multiple trips. They've paid for themselves many times over.
author: ConsultantLife

title: Good not great for the gym
review_text: Tried using these for workouts. They're too warm and not sweat resistant at all. They slip when I'm doing intense cardio. Great for light walks and commutes but definitely not workout headphones. Stick with earbuds for the gym.
author: GymRat_Jake

title: Impressive voice assistant integration
review_text: Google Assistant and Alexa work flawlessly. I can control my smart home, check weather, and manage reminders hands-free. The voice pickup is good enough that I don't have to shout. Love the quick attention mode — just cover the right ear cup.
author: SmartHomeSam

title: Zero regrets at full price
review_text: I rarely buy electronics at full MSRP but these are worth it. The combination of sound quality, ANC, comfort, and battery life is unmatched. This is my third pair of Sony flagships and they keep getting better. Instant recommendation to anyone who asks.
author: DayOneBuyer

title: Auto-pause feature is unreliable
review_text: The wear detection sensor constantly thinks I've taken them off. Music pauses randomly mid-song. Apparently hair can trigger the sensor. I had to disable the feature entirely which is frustrating because it should be a useful feature.
author: CurlyHairCurse

title: Fantastic for working from home
review_text: Working remotely for 3 years and these are the best headphones I've owned for the job. ANC blocks out my neighbor's construction noise. Call quality is good enough for daily standups and client meetings. Comfortable for 6+ hour days.
author: RemoteDev_Lisa

title: The king is back
review_text: Sony reclaims the throne. Better ANC than Bose QC Ultra. Better sound than Apple AirPods Max. More features than Sennheiser Momentum 4. And at a lower price than most competitors. This is the headphone to beat in 2024.
author: TechReviewerAI

title: Wish it had wired ANC mode
review_text: When the battery dies, you can use the included cable but ANC turns off. So you're left with passive isolation only which isn't great with the shallow ear cups. Every competitor should support wired+ANC mode like some Bose models do.
author: PowerUserPat

title: Great for meditation and sleep
review_text: I use these with sleep sounds and meditation apps. The ANC creates complete silence which is perfect for deep meditation. They're comfortable enough to wear lying down if you're on your back. Battery lasts days with this kind of use.
author: MindfulMark

title: Second pair because first ones lasted 3 years
review_text: My XM5s finally gave out after 3 years of daily heavy use. That's impressive longevity. Immediately bought another pair because nothing else comes close. The only headphone I'm loyal to.
author: RepeatCustomer

title: Not enough bass for hip-hop
review_text: If you listen to bass-heavy genres, these might disappoint. The default tuning is balanced/neutral which is great for rock and classical but hip-hop and EDM feel anemic. Yes you can boost bass in EQ but then it sounds muddy. Not ideal.
author: BassHead_Mike

title: Kids love the speak-to-chat
review_text: My 14-year-old never takes headphones off. At least with speak-to-chat, when I call their name the music pauses and I can talk to them. Brilliant feature for parents of teens. Sound quality is excellent according to my kid.
author: ParentReview

title: Professional musician approved
review_text: I play violin in an orchestra and these are my go-to for practice. The sound reproduction is accurate enough to hear my mistakes. ANC helps me practice in my apartment without hearing my noisy neighbors. Highly recommended for musicians.
author: ViolinistV

title: Good headphones terrible case design
review_text: The headphones are great. But the new case is awful — it's bigger because the headphones don't fold, and the zipper quality is cheap. My XM4 case was much better. Small detail but it matters for daily carry.
author: DetailDevil

title: Excellent value at sale price
review_text: Picked these up during Prime Day for $278 and at that price they're an absolute steal. Every feature works as advertised. ANC is class-leading. Sound is rich and detailed. Would be a solid buy even at full price.
author: DealHunter

title: Noise cancellation is witchcraft
review_text: Genuinely concerned Sony is using dark magic. Sat in a coffee shop with a screaming toddler two tables over and heard absolutely nothing. My partner thought I was ignoring them but I literally could not hear them speaking. ANC is that good.
author: MagicBeliever
